label: plain_text
text:
Brahmagupta
(c. 628
CE)
formalised
the
notion
and
use
of
zero
and t
the 1
negative
numbers as a
algebraic
entities; in
modern
coordinate
systems, the ‘origin' is
s zero and the ‘negative axes'
represent values less
than
zero. Without
Brahmagupta's
work, the
four-quadrant
Cartesian
plane, as we will
 study in this chapter, would be impossible.
